question: Q-federated-hswm-merge-crossfield
conjecture: On 2Wiki (n_q=400, title-parity two-field split), the seam-bound merged field beats per-query best-single on cross-field queries (recall@10 delta > noise band 0.02 with bootstrap95 lower > 0), does not harm in-field queries beyond the noise band (L5), and the gain vanishes without seam arcs (F-B2c ablation, delta > 0).

already_public_before_registration.note: Input stratification stats only were inspected before registration: 500 rows all usable, cross_field 298 / in_field 202. No arm output (recall, delta, CI, seam count effect) was computed or inspected. ML8 hswm-of-hswms coverage win is NOT evidence here (ML10 proved it a metric artifact) — hence paired bootstrap + simultaneous no-harm requirement.
already_public_before_registration.harness_provenance: built by subagent under no-measurement constraint; parent re-verified 18 tests green and determinism

kill_conditions: f_b2a bootstrap95 lower <= 0 or delta <= noise_band -> cross-field merge payoff unconfirmed; federated network claim stays open at best.
f_b2b in-field delta < -0.02 -> L5 no-harm violated; merge interferes (ML15 noise-bridge recurrence), network claim REJECTED regardless of f_b2a.
f_b2c delta <= 0 -> gain (if any) is not carried by seam binding; the 'connection' mechanism claim dies even if merged wins.
usable rows < n_q or any gold consumption during field construction -> measurement void.

scope_boundary.dataset: 2Wiki 500-row frozen sidecar only; second benchmark and seeds sweep deferred
scope_boundary.claims: field-level merge payoff only; no reasoner or kernel-traversal claim

user_canon_seed: USER 2026-07-22 'SOLID 한 여러개 HSWM 의 연결로 큰 HSWM 망' (KG user-canon-federated-hswm-network-2026-07-22)
